perturbation.original_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” means information concerning the Provider which has been or is furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives which contain or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by the Provider hereunder. The term Evaluation Material does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within the Recipient’s possession prior to its being furnished to the Recipient by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that the source of such information was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Provider or its Representatives, provided that such source is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such information or (iv) is or has been independently developed by an employee, agent or contractor of the Recipient without use or reference to any information furnished by Provider or its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Evaluation Material ” means data concerning the Provider which has been or is furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives (as defined below) in connection with the Recipient’s evaluation of a Possible Transaction, including its business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities, and includes all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by the Recipient or its Representatives which contain or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by the Provider hereunder. The term Evaluation Material does not include data which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Recipient or its Representatives in breach of this Agreement, (ii) was within the Recipient’s possession prior to its being furnished to the Recipient by or on behalf of the Provider, provided that the source of such information was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such data, (iii) is or becomes available to the Recipient on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the Provider or its Representatives, provided that such source is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to, the Provider with respect to such data or (iv) is or has been independently developed by an employee, agent or contractor of the Recipient without use or reference to any information furnished by Provider or its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'data' within the definition of 'Evaluation Material' introduces a subtle but significant ambiguity. While seemingly interchangeable, 'data' often implies structured, digital information, whereas 'information' is broader and includes unstructured materials. This switch can cause disputes in later sections. For example, if the Provider furnishes physical documents, a Recipient might argue that these are not covered because 'Evaluation Material' is now defined as 'data' only.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If either Company decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it will promptly inform the other Company of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the request of a Provider for any reason, a Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to, within five business days after receipt of such notice or request, destroy or return all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained. The Recipient shall provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with the previous sentence signed by an executive officer of the Recipient. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by such Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'information' with 'data' in the definition of 'Evaluation Material'. The Contradicted Text requires the Recipient to return or destroy 'all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider'. If 'Evaluation Material' is now limited to 'data' (i.e., structured, digital information), then the Recipient might argue that physical documents or unstructured information are not included in this obligation, which directly contradicts the requirement to return or destroy 'all' Evaluation Material, creating a direct conflict and ambiguity, thus, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The term “ Representatives ” shall include the directors, officers, employees, agents, partners or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers and financial advisors) of the Recipient or Provider, as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Representatives ” shall include the directors, officers, employees, agents, partners or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers and financial advisors) of the Recipient only, as applicable.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting 'Representatives' to only include those of the 'Recipient' and not the 'Provider', it creates confusion in the context of shared responsibilities or actions that require coordination between both parties' representatives. For example, Section 5 requires the Recipient to notify the Provider if its Representatives are compelled to disclose Evaluation Material. But if 'Representatives' are now only those of the Recipient, it's unclear if the Provider's representatives are bound by the same confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to Section 5, Potential Buyer agrees that, without the prior written consent of Convio, Potential Buyer will not, and will cause its Representatives not to, disclose to any other Person (i) that Evaluation Material has been exchanged between the Companies, (ii) that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Companies concerning a Possible Transaction, or (iii) any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof). Potential Buyer acknowledges that Convio is free to disclose (i) that Evaluation Material has been exchanged between the Companies or (ii) that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Companies concerning a Possible Transaction; provided that Convio does not, and causes its Representatives not to, disclose to any other Person the identity of Potential Buyer or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to a Possible Transaction except to the extent permitted by Section 5 or as otherwise believed reasonably required or necessary to comply with the fiduciary duties or other obligations applicable to Convio and its directors, officers and employees or with other applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the definition of 'Representatives' to only include those of the 'Recipient', excluding the 'Provider'. The contradicted text refers to 'Potential Buyer' and its 'Representatives' in the context of confidentiality obligations regarding 'Evaluation Material'. The original text included both Recipient and Provider as having Representatives, which aligned with the contradicted text. By excluding 'Provider' representatives, it creates a contradiction because the Potential Buyer (Recipient) is now obligated to control only its own Representatives, creating ambiguity as to whether the Provider's Representatives are bound by the same confidentiality obligations mentioned in the contradicted text. This directly conflicts with the intent of the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Each Recipient shall, and it shall cause its Representatives to, use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will cause its Representatives not to , disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction. Each Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of such Recipient’s Representatives. This Agreement does not grant a Recipient or any of its Representatives any license to use the Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Recipient shall, and it shall cause its Agents to, use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, keep the Evaluation Material confidential, and, subject to Section 5, will not, and will cause its Agents not to , disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s Agents who need to know such information for the sole purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction. Each Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of such Recipient’s Agents. This Agreement does not grant a Recipient or any of its Agents any license to use the Provider’s Evaluation Material except as provided herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Representatives' to 'Agents' creates an inconsistency. The definition of 'Representatives' in Section 2(b) is broad (directors, officers, employees, etc.), while 'Agents' generally implies a narrower, legally defined role with specific authorities. This change creates uncertainty in enforcement. The Recipient might argue that certain individuals who were previously covered as 'Representatives' (e.g., consultants) are no longer bound by the same obligations because they don't qualify as 'Agents'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term “ Representatives ” shall include the directors, officers, employees, agents, partners or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers and financial advisors) of the Recipient or Provider, as applicable.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'Representatives' which, according to the contradicted text, includes directors, officers, employees, agents, partners, advisors (including attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers and financial advisors). The changed text replaces 'Representatives' with 'Agents'. This creates a contradiction because 'Agents' has a narrower legal definition than 'Representatives'. The explanation states that the Recipient might argue that certain individuals who were previously covered as 'Representatives' (e.g., consultants) are no longer bound by the same obligations because they don't qualify as 'Agents'. This directly contradicts the original intent and scope, causing a strong in-text contradiction by narrowing the scope in one location but not the other.

perturbation.original_text: Potential Buyer agrees that, without the prior written consent of Convio, Potential Buyer will not, and will cause its Representatives not to, disclose to any other Person (i) that Evaluation Material has been exchanged between the Companies, (ii) that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Companies concerning a Possible Transaction, or (iii) any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof). Potential Buyer acknowledges that Convio is free to disclose (i) that Evaluation Material has been exchanged between the Companies or (ii) that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Companies concerning a Possible Transaction; provided that Convio does not, and causes its Representatives not to, disclose to any other Person the identity of Potential Buyer or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to a Possible Transaction except to the extent permitted by Section 5 or as otherwise believed reasonably required or necessary to comply with the fiduciary duties or other obligations applicable to Convio and its directors, officers and employees or with other applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: Potential Buyer agrees that, without the prior written consent of Convio, Potential Buyer will not, and will cause its Affiliates not to, disclose to any other Person (i) that Evaluation Material has been exchanged between the Companies, (ii) that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Companies concerning a Possible Transaction, or (iii) any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto (including the status thereof). Potential Buyer acknowledges that Convio is free to disclose (i) that Evaluation Material has been exchanged between the Companies or (ii) that discussions or negotiations are taking place between the Companies concerning a Possible Transaction; provided that Convio does not, and causes its Representatives not to, disclose to any other Person the identity of Potential Buyer or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to a Possible Transaction except to the extent permitted by Section 5 or as otherwise believed reasonably required or necessary to comply with the fiduciary duties or other obligations applicable to Convio and its directors, officers and employees or with other applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Representatives' to 'Affiliates' in Section 4 introduces confusion regarding the scope of non-disclosure obligations. 'Representatives' is a defined term (Section 2(b)), encompassing a broad range of individuals and entities. 'Affiliates', while often used in legal agreements, lacks a precise definition within *this* contract. Later, Section 8 defines affiliate for the 'Standstill' section using the Exchange Act of 1934, but not for general use. This inconsistency makes it unclear whether the non-disclosure obligation extends only to legally defined affiliates, or to the broader group initially intended by 'Representatives'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term “ Representatives ” shall include the directors, officers, employees, agents, partners or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers and financial advisors) of the Recipient or Provider, as applicable.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Representatives' to 'Affiliates' in the Changed Text introduces a contradiction with the definition of 'Representatives' provided in the Contradicted Text. The original text clearly defines 'Representatives' as including a broad range of individuals and entities. The Changed Text uses 'Affiliates', which is undefined in the general contract terms but defined elsewhere using a narrower definition (Exchange Act of 1934), making it unclear if the non-disclosure obligation extends to the same group as originally intended. This creates ambiguity and a potential contradiction, as the Contradicted Text provides a specific definition that is not compatible with the changed term 'Affiliates' and it's unclear scope.

perturbation.original_text: If a Recipient or its Representatives are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, other requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited under Section 4 above, such Recipient shall provide the Provider with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement together with copies of the material proposed to be disclosed so that the Provider may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Provider, a Recipient or any of its Representatives is nonetheless legally compelled to disclose Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited under Section 4 or would otherwise be liable for contempt or suffer other censure or penalty, such Recipient or its Representatives may, without liability hereunder, disclose to such requiring Person only that portion of such Evaluation Material or any such facts which the Recipient or its Representatives is legally required to disclose, provided that the Recipient and/or its Representatives cooperate with the Provider to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Material or such facts by the Person receiving the material.
perturbation.changed_text: If a Recipient or its Personnel are requested or required (by oral questions, interrogatories, other requests for information or documents in legal proceedings, subpoena, civil investigative demand or other similar process) to disclose any of the Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited under Section 4 above, such Recipient shall provide the Provider with prompt written notice of any such request or requirement together with copies of the material proposed to be disclosed so that the Provider may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. If, in the absence of a protective order or other remedy or the receipt of a waiver by the Provider, a Recipient or any of its Personnel is nonetheless legally compelled to disclose Evaluation Material or any of the facts disclosure of which is prohibited under Section 4 or would otherwise be liable for contempt or suffer other censure or penalty, such Recipient or its Personnel may, without liability hereunder, disclose to such requiring Person only that portion of such Evaluation Material or any such facts which the Recipient or its Personnel is legally required to disclose, provided that the Recipient and/or its Personnel cooperate with the Provider to obtain an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Material or such facts by the Person receiving the material.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Representatives' to 'Personnel' introduces ambiguity because 'Personnel' is undefined. Section 2(b) defines 'Representatives,' so this change creates a contradiction. 'Personnel' could be interpreted as only direct employees, excluding advisors or consultants who were intended to be included under the original definition of 'Representatives'. This could lead to a scenario where a consultant is legally compelled to disclose information, and the Recipient argues they were not obligated to provide notice because the consultant is not 'Personnel'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term “ Representatives ” shall include the directors, officers, employees, agents, partners or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers and financial advisors) of the Recipient or Provider, as applicable.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Representatives' to 'Personnel' creates a contradiction because 'Representatives' is a defined term in the contract, specifically including advisors and consultants, while 'Personnel' is not defined and could be interpreted more narrowly to only include direct employees. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states the definition of Representatives includes advisors, as the change now potentially excludes those advisors from the obligation to provide notice, leading to uncertainty and a strong potential contradiction in obligation between the two sections based on terminology. As per the definition of Misaligned Terminology, this change creates an in-text contradiction because 'Personnel' is used in place of a clearly defined 'Representatives', leading to conflicting obligations of what is to be done.
